title: Defining vulnerable populationspopulations – Closed consultation - Canada.ca
content:
## Defining vulnerable populations – Closed consultation

## Current status: Closed
This consultation ran from November 22, 2018 to January 21, 2019.
We were seeking input on a proposed definition of vulnerable populations within the context of chemicals management.  This was a first step towards the development of a policy framework focussed on enhancing the protection of vulnerable populations through the assessment and management of risks associated with certain chemicals, in particular under the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 (CEPA 1999).

## Who  is the focus of this consultation
- Health non-governmental groups
- health professionals and associated groups
- academia and associated groups
- industry members and associated groups

## Key  question for discussion
Ideas and inputs were sought regarding the following definition of vulnerable populations:
“a  group of individuals within the general Canadian population who, due to either  greater susceptibility and/or exposure, may be at greater risk than the general  population of experiencing adverse health effects from exposure to  chemicals"
The input that was gathered through this process will be used to finalize this definition.
- A summary of feedback from the consultation: Defining vulnerable populations - A first step toward a policy framework on vulnerable populations
Existing Substances Risk Assessment Bureau Safe Environments Directorate Healthy Environments and Consumer Safety Branch Health Canada 269 Laurier Ave. West A.L. 4904A Ottawa, Ontario K1A 0K9 E-mail: hc.esrabdirector-directeurberse.sc@canada.ca
